title: 10 best herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with high blood pressure because of its unique combination of natural ingredients. The cream contains Adiantum, a potent herb that has been traditionally used to lower blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving circulation. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, common contributing factors to hypertension. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, Maidenhair tree creams can help regulate blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract contains antioxidants that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilation: The plant's active compounds may help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which could potentially lower blood pressure.
Cardiovascular protection: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antiplatelet and antithrombotic effects, which may reduce the risk of cardiovascular events such as heart attacks and strokes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving vascular function.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain terpenes and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat hypertension and other cardiovascular conditions due to their ability to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 3 hours.
Add 20 drops of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well to combine within 1 minute.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have a profound impact on cardiovascular health. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, reduce blood vessel stiffness, and improve blood flow, all of which can help to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with hypertension. Additionally, hawthorn extracts have been shown to decrease the cardiac workload, reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn cream contains flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and increase vasodilation, helping to lower blood pressure.
Oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn cream have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn cream contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce the workload of the heart, both of which can help alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
Barks: Contains oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which have antioxidant properties that help to reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers in early morning when dew still present and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers with 100 grams of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steam distill the mixture at 150°f for 30 minutes to extract essential oils and medicinal compounds from plant material.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids within an hour of straining.
Mix 50 grams of the infused oil with 25 grams of beeswax and heat gently over low flame for 10-15 minutes until smooth and creamy consistency achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds that have a potent effect on blood vessel dilation, which in turn reduces blood pressure. The cream's active ingredients, such as allicin and s-allyl cysteine, work to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing resistance, thereby lowering blood pressure. Additionally, herbal garlic creams may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, further contributing to their hypotensive effects, making them a valuable adjunctive therapy for individuals with high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Herbal garlic creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessel walls and increasing blood vessel constriction.
Nitric oxide boosting: Garlic in herbal creams helps increase nitric oxide production, which dilates blood vessels, lowers blood pressure, and improves blood flow to the heart.
Vasodilation promotion: The compounds in garlic cream, such as allicin, help relax blood vessels, leading to improved vasodilation, reduced peripheral resistance, and lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots contain a high concentration of allicin, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the garlic plant, and it contains compounds that have vasodilatory effects, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried garlic powder or fresh garlic cloves of allium sativum and measure out 100g for infusion.
Combine the measured garlic with 500ml distilled water in a double boiler and steep for 20-30 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids and reserving infused liquid.
Mix the infused garlic liquid with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl and melt over low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir well to combine, and let cool to 40°c before pouring into sterilized jars, sealing tightly, and labeling properly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have potent antihypertensive properties. This natural extract has been found to relax and dilate blood vessels, allowing for easier blood flow and lowering of blood pressure. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that often contribute to high blood pressure. By using herbal licorice creams, individuals may experience a reduction in their blood pressure levels over time.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, ultimately lowering blood pressure.

Glycyrrhizin: The main compound in licorice root, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to have a direct effect on the body's ability to regulate blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme that converts angiotensin I to angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor.

Inositol: Licorice creams often contain inositol, a compound that helps regulate blood vessel tone and function, improving circulation and reducing peripheral resistance, which can contribute to lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and mix it with 100 milliliters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Combine the mixture with 50 grams of sweet almond oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and warm it gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius before adding 10 milliliters of tea tree essential oil and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in a dark place for at least 2 weeks before using, allowing the cream to mature and thicken.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper that has been shown to have a positive impact on blood pressure levels. Piperine is believed to help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, both of which can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, piperine may also help to increase nitric oxide production, which can further aid in improving cardiovascular health. As a result, incorporating herbal black pepper creams into one's daily routine may be a natural way to support healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has been shown to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and reducing vascular resistance.

Antioxidant Properties: Herbal black pepper creams containing antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal black pepper creams, such as caryophyllene and limonene, may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of black pepper plant have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their vasodilatory properties.
Barks: The barks of black pepper contain piperine, a potent compound that helps reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried black pepper fruits or piper nigrum berries from a trusted source.
Grind the dried piper nigrum berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered piper nigrum berry extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 120°f and remains at that temperature for 5 minutes before cooling.
Pour the cooled herbal cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. The natural antihypertensive properties of the herbs help to reduce blood pressure by lowering peripheral resistance, allowing blood to flow more easily through the vessels. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in bell pepper creams may also help to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is a major contributor to high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by improving blood vessel function and reducing oxidative stress.
Potassium: Bell peppers contain potassium, a mineral that can help counterbalance the effects of sodium in the body and regulate blood pressure.
Fiber: The fiber content in bell peppers may also contribute to its potential benefits for high blood pressure by helping to regulate blood sugar levels and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat hypertension due to their ability to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
Seeds: Bell pepper seeds contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have been shown to help reduce blood pressure by improving blood vessel function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and flowers for infusion in boiling water.
Combine 10g of dried capsicum annuum powder with 20ml of jojoba oil and 10ml of coconut oil in a heatproof bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and stir occasionally as it cools to 40°c over 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and labeling with date and contents.
Store the cream in the refrigerator at 5-10°c for up to 6 months or freeze at -20°c for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with high blood pressure because it contains powerful phytochemicals that work to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood flow and lower blood pressure. Additionally, burdock root has been shown to have a diuretic effect, helping to remove excess fluid from the body and further reducing blood pressure.

Rhyzomes: The underground stem (rhyzome) of burdock contains a compound called lactone, which has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh arctium lappa roots and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind 30g of dried arctium lappa into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered arctium lappa with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler until melted completely.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly then add 2% vitamin e oil as an antioxidant and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator at 5°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cardiovascular health. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried panax ginseng root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground ginseng powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler for infusion.

Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter for cream formation.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a unique combination of herbal extracts that work synergistically to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying factors contributing to hypertension. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax blood vessels, improving circulation and lowering blood pressure readings. remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb creams help with high blood pressure because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work synergistically to reduce blood pressure. The creams' active ingredients, including anthraquinones and sennosides, help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing resistance in the circulatory system. This natural vasodilation effect helps to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure readings, alleviating symptoms of hypertension and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Viscoelastic properties: Chinese Rhubarb creams have viscoelastic properties that help to increase blood viscosity, which can improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure by reducing peripheral resistance.

Astringent and antispasmodic effects: The astringent and antispasmodic properties of Chinese Rhubarb creams help to relax blood vessels, reducing the tone of blood vessel walls and improving blood flow, thereby lowering blood pressure.

Diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects: The diuretic properties of Chinese Rhubarb creams can help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention, which can alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure such as swelling and bloating. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects may also help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a contributing factor to hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chinese rhubarb roots are valued for their ability to promote blood circulation, reduce blood viscosity, and lower blood pressure, making them an effective ingredient in creams for hypertension treatment.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of Chinese rhubarb is used due to its diuretic properties, which help remove excess fluids from the body, reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms associated with hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rheum palmatum roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 hours.
Mix the ground rheum palmatum powder with distilled water in a large bowl until it forms a smooth paste consistency of 20 grams per 100 milliliters.
Combine the paste with coconut oil, shea butter, and vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture well and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage at ambient temperatures.
Allow the cream to mature for 14 days before using it topically, applying a thin layer to affected areas twice daily for 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldthread creams helps with high blood pressure because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and vasodilators. The goldthread plant contains a powerful compound called berberine, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. remedy_properties: Cortex Coptidis: Cortex Coptidis, also known as Goldthread (Coptis chinensis), has been shown to have a vasodilating effect, which can help to relax and widen blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure.

Berberine: Berberine, a bioactive compound found in Goldthread, has been demonstrated to exhibit anti-hypertensive properties by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key player in regulating blood pressure.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds present in Goldthread creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension, thereby supporting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain berberine, a compound that helps lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.

remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried coptis chinensis root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered coptis chinensis with 200ml of glycerin and 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before adding 20g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil, blending until smooth and creamy.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store in a refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which can help to relax and widen blood vessels. Rhyzomes: Red shiso rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, contributing to a reduction in blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder 1000mg, coconut oil 200ml and beeswax pellets 50g.
Combine lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder and coconut oil in a double boiler whisking until smooth and well combined taking approximately 10 minutes.
Add beeswax pellets to the mixture stirring constantly until fully incorporated and melted requiring approximately 5 minutes more.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly for 5-7 minutes before pouring into a sterilized glass jar or container.
Allow the cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use or storage in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a potent combination of natural ingredients that work synergistically to promote cardiovascular health. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring adequate quality control.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator set at 70°f for 6-8 hours or sunlight for 3 days.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to release active compounds.
Mix 1 part bacopa monnieri powder with 2 parts coconut oil and 0.5 parts beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into sterilized jars, storing in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian snakeroot creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds like beraprost, which help to relax and dilate blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried rauvolfia serpentina root powder from a reputable supplier.
Mix the powder with 10ml of jojoba oil in a glass container for 24 hours at room temperature.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently until the wax dissolves completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.
Package the herbal cream in tamper-evident tubes or jars with tight-fitting lids and label them with date of preparation and ingredients used.

Combine 200g of coconut oil with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts completely after 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean asparagus racemosus roots ensuring they are free from dirt and debris.
Grind the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 200g of asparagus racemosus root powder with 1000ml of distilled water to create a decoction for 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for high blood pressure are topical solutions infused with natural ingredients that help to lower blood pressure levels when applied directly to the skin. These creams contain herbs like hawthorn, garlic, and licorice root, which have been shown to improve circulation, relax blood vessels, and reduce stress hormones that contribute to hypertension. Examples of herbal creams include Garlic & Hawthorn Cream and Blood Pressure Relief Cream, which can help individuals with high blood pressure manage their condition more effectively, reducing the risk of heart disease and stroke while improving overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat high blood pressure with herbal creams is to combine them with a healthy lifestyle. Look for creams containing herbs like hawthorn, garlic, and ginkgo biloba, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure naturally. Apply the cream topically to the skin, typically twice daily, allowing it to absorb fully before dressing. Consult with a healthcare professional to ensure proper dosing and monitor progress regularly.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high blood pressure that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's disease, and venous insufficiency. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, arnica, and horse chestnut may help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and swelling. These natural remedies can provide an alternative or complementary approach to conventional treatments for these conditions.
